ag_detail: AG: federal grants = 49.0% of FY24 revenue, single largest source; our checkbook-cash measure (~45%) lands within methodology gap (ACFR accrual vs cash).
AG: AHCCCS = 57.3% ($16.4B) of all federal spend; financials restated for errors; 300+ sober-living providers suspended for fraudulent behavioral-health billing since May 2023.
AG: DES UI bank recs incomplete (fraud risk), $257.4M COVID UI unsupported, childcare/rental payments lacked eligibility docs. DES = 13% ($3.7B) of federal spend, 6 findings (most of any agency).
AG: ADE used improper procurement (no fair/open competition) on Education Stabilization Fund; unauthorized employees signed a noncompetitive waiver.
AG: Corrections (DCRR) spent $50.9M opioid-settlement monies without documentation of allowable use.
AG independently found DEMA lacked pay-rate documentation on $1.7M federal payroll — the exact category our data cannot see; confirms a real gap our feed cannot cover.
AG: ACFR filed ~13 months late; uncorrected federal findings rose 8 (FY19) -> 42 (FY24). Independent confirmation that control quality is deteriorating, not improving.

AG accrual ACFR: State net position INCREASED $259M in FY24 (rev > exp). Our cash-basis proxy diverges ~$2.2B; the FY24 "watch" must NOT be read as accrual deterioration. Exactly the cash-vs-GAAP caveat in our methodology.
